danger_signals.visual_form: Animated food creatures (apples, toasters, etc.) with hostile behavior patterns
danger_signals.meaning: Restaurant is cursed/invaded; danger comes from corrupted culinary elements

danger_signals.visual_form: Large-scale food enemy variants positioned as stage barriers
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating threat progression; player must defeat increasingly dangerous manifestations

danger_signals.visual_form: Status bar display showing health/vitality meter
danger_signals.meaning: Player vulnerability state; visual feedback on cumulative damage

danger_signals.visual_form: Cookie armed only with basic attack (headbutt in JP, pan-smash in western version)
danger_signals.meaning: Vulnerability periods between weapon pickups create danger windows

safety_signals.visual_form: Health candy and health-extending lollipops scattered through levels
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones contain restorative resources; visual markers of respite opportunities

safety_signals.visual_form: Kitchen utensil items (spoon, fork, plate, frying pan, cooking pot)
safety_signals.meaning: Empowerment moments; armed state provides safety advantage against enemies

safety_signals.visual_form: Life-granting collectibles positioned strategically through levels
safety_signals.meaning: Second-chance insurance; safety net against resource depletion

safety_signals.visual_form: Final stage location representing owned/controlled space
safety_signals.meaning: Destination safety; progression toward reclamation of homeland

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Chef/cook identity (young in JP, older man in western version)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Kitchen utensils as weapon extensions; restaurant ownership identity

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Commands corrupted sentient food as proxy army
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence marked by food infestation throughout six level progression

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Animated culinary elements (apples, toasters, bubble vats, ice platforms)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple food-type enemies with distinct behaviors per level theme
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Level theming: Appetizer → Soup → Salad → Fish → Meat → Dessert creates faction visual hierarchy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Six themed level stages representing corrupted food categories; sentient food enemies populate each
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively patrol and attack; Cookie must fight through each themed area to progress

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage layouts modified between JP and western versions to increase difficulty; item placement shifts create resource scarcity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss encounters require specific weapon strategies; regional difficulty adjustments change threat intensity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Final stage set inside EatEn restaurant (owned space); Ohdove confrontation as narrative climax
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Victory condition unlocks ending sequence; regional variants show credits or immediate end state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character redesign (young chef → older man); weapon changes (headbutt → pan smash); attack power-up variants (black frying pan → cooking pot)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Title screen graphics modified; Disco credit removal; western-specific difficulty tuning; PAL overscan accommodation

environmental_storytelling.example: Six stages named Appetizer, Soup, Salad, Fish, Meat, Dessert create narrative of progressing through restaurant menu structure; each level visually represents its food type through enemy and obstacle design
Final boss encounter occurs inside the EatEn restaurant itself, signaling that player has fought through external contamination to reach the core conflict location
Spoon, fork, plate, pan, and pot as combat tools reinforce chef/culinary theme; weapons are environmental tools repurposed for battle, embedding world logic into mechanics

environmental_storytelling.technique: Cursed food as visual invasion metaphor
environmental_storytelling.example: Sentient food enemies represent corruption of chef's domain; food becoming hostile inverts the safe/nourishing kitchen space into threatening territory

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Capable of offensive action against enemies; can defeat food enemies and progress through levels
Vulnerable; basic attack less effective; should seek weapon pickups to restore combat effectiveness
Pursue and attack Cookie; force player to engage combat or navigate around them
Remove threat; potentially drop health or weapon items upon destruction
Requires sustained combat engagement; blocks progression to next level; indicates stage completion threshold
Ultimate threat requiring defeat to achieve game victory; represents narrative climax and curse-breaking moment

notes: Panic Restaurant is a simple platformer with minimal political/faction complexity. World-state communication relies heavily on thematic coherence (food-based enemies, culinary setting) and difficulty progression rather than faction signaling or geopolitical messaging. Regional localization differences are primarily cosmetic (character age, weapon names) rather than mechanically altering world-state communication. Danger/safety signals are communicated through standard platformer conventions: health bars, enemy presence, item availability. The 'world state' is essentially binary: restaurant cursed (stages 1-6) → restaurant reclaimed (ending). No dynamic faction allegiances or political alignment systems present.
